holdings.name: Vedanta
holdings.reasoning: MSCI Global Standard index removal effective June 22 creates a concrete near-term forced-selling overhang as passive funds must exit the residual Vedanta entity; short-term direction is highly uncertain around this event. The demerger did unlock value overall (combined entities +18% vs pre-demerger), but the residual VEDL stock faces structural index-exclusion pressure that makes adding near this event risky. Hold existing position rather than add, and reassess post-MSCI rebalancing — this is information only, not advice.

holdings.name: State Bank of India
holdings.reasoning: SBI's Q4 FY25 net profit fell ~10% YoY to ₹18,643 cr due to absence of one-time provision write-backs and rising provisions, while domestic NIM compressed to ~3.15% (down 32 bps YoY) — signals a tougher profitability cycle ahead. Full-year FY25 PAT of ₹70,901 cr (+16% YoY) is a strong headline, but the board has approved a ₹25,000 cr equity capital raise (FPO/QIP) in FY26, which creates near-term dilution overhang. At ₹1,026.5, the stock is off its highs and options flow suggests the ₹1,000 strike is acting as a near-term cap — short-term direction is genuinely uncertain; wait for clarity on dilution timing and NIM stabilization before entering.

holdings.name: ITC
holdings.reasoning: ITC has shed ~30% over the past year, weighed down by a steep government cigarette tax hike (20–55%) notified in late 2024 that directly pressures its core profit engine; MOSL maintains a cautious view citing ongoing tax/earnings pressure and sluggish volume growth. The Hotels demerger (effective Jan 2025) has unlocked some value but has not arrested the stock's decline toward current multi-year lows near ₹285–290. At ₹290.75, a floor may be forming technically, but with regulatory/taxation overhang unresolved, buying conviction is low — wait for clearer evidence of cigarette volume resilience or tax clarity before entering.

holdings.name: JSW Infrastructure
holdings.reasoning: JSW Infra shows solid long-term fundamentals — ~20% revenue growth in FY26, a 30-year Kolkata NSD terminal concession win, and a ₹6,000 cr QIP plan to fund a ₹39,000 cr capex roadmap to 400 MTPA by FY30 — but Q4 FY26 reported PAT fell ~18% QoQ due to exceptional items (fire loss at Fujairah) and margin pressure, which creates near-term overhang. The QIP, while growth-positive, is a known dilution event whose price and timing are uncertain, and the stock at ₹299 is trading well above the post-results low of ~₹274, suggesting the QIP news is partly priced in. Beating NIFTY on any single day is close to a coin flip; entry here is defensible only for patient, long-horizon buyers who can absorb dilution risk.

items.risk: Any RBI tightening on consumer credit or a rise in stage-2/3 assets in the unsecured book could trigger a sharp de-rating

items.name: NTPC Ltd
items.why: Power sector tailwind from India's record summer peak-demand; NTPC's renewable capacity addition pipeline (50 GW target by 2032) and government capex backing provide a medium-term re-rating catalyst distinct from POWERGRID (which you already hold)
items.risk: Fuel-cost pass-through delays and slower-than-expected renewable commissioning could disappoint near-term earnings expectations

items.name: Maruti Suzuki India

items.risk: Any sharp INR depreciation raises royalty/import-component costs, and a demand slowdown in rural India would disproportionately hurt volumes
